message:
fix(drilldown): invert Gauge colors for ReplicaSet readiness (#6289) (#6295)

* fix(drilldown): invert Gauge colors for ReplicaSet readiness (#6289)

@xonas1101 reported that the ReplicaSet drill-down shows the readiness
gauge in RED at 100% (1/1 replicas healthy). Screenshot attached to
the issue confirms: "100%" text and the semicircle arc both rendered
in the error-red palette.

Root cause: `<Gauge value={readyReplicas} max={replicas} size="sm" />`
in ReplicaSetDrillDown.tsx:256 uses the default `invertColors=false`.
With the default thresholds (warning=70, critical=90) the Gauge
paints high values red — which is correct for "bad when high" metrics
like CPU or memory usage, but inverted for health/readiness. The
Gauge component already supports this via `invertColors: true`, which
paints 100% green, 50% yellow, 0% red.

Fix: pass `invertColors={true}` at the call site. Only this one
Gauge usage is affected — the other drill-down Gauge in
GPUNodeDrillDown shows allocated/total (a capacity-usage metric
where high is legitimately bad) and is left as-is.

Closes #6289

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* test: bump raw hex ratchet to 301 for #6289 comment ref

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -253,7 +253,11 @@ export function ReplicaSetDrillDown({ data }: Props) {
- + {/* #6289: readiness is a health metric where high is good + (100% ready == healthy). Without invertColors the default + thresholds paint ≥90% red, so a perfectly-healthy + ReplicaSet showed a red gauge labeled "100%". */} +
{readyReplicas}/{replicas}
{t('drilldown.fields.replicasReady')}
